one_line_takeaway: SpatialJB shows that rearranging harmful text into human-readable 2D spatial layouts (acrostic, diagonal, corner, etc.) reliably defeats sequential-token-based output guardrails, driving ASR to near 100% on several LLMs versus under 16% for existing encoding/language-based jailbreak baselines, and the paper's own fixed-position-extraction defense (SpatialD) only partially closes the gap.
key_finding: Without any guardrail, SpatialJB reaches up to 100% ASR on DeepSeek-V3, Llama4, and Grok4 for the Repeat task (Table 1), and 91-97% on GPT-4/Gemini 2.5-Pro; the weakest target is Claude at 62.7-79.7% depending on template. The three baseline jailbreaks (Zulu, Base64, Ubbi Dubbi) only reach 4.7-15.6% ASR across all settings -- an 80+ percentage-point gap. Under output guardrails, ASR drops but SpatialJB remains dominant: e.g., against Claude with Llama Guard 4 the Acrostic-template ASR falls to 57%, and under the strictest guardrail tested (Perspective API) Claude's ASR ranges 61-77.7% while DeepSeek-V3/Grok4 remain 78.3-93%+ across all six templates. On the harder Summary task (Table 2), ASR is generally 15-40 points lower across the board (e.g., Claude drops to 42.8-59.8% under Llama Guard) but SpatialJB still vastly outperforms baselines (baselines stay in the 2.9-9.7% range). The ablation (Table 3) shows ASR against Claude climbing from 68% (format control alone) to 97.6% when combining format control + Positive Guidance + Think Guidance, described by the authors as an approximately linear/additive improvement. The SpatialD defense achieves Defense Success Rate averaging 90.5% (Acrostic) down to 77% (Staircase) across five LLMs (Table 4), with the authors noting it depends on fixed, pre-programmed extraction positions and would likely degrade against attacks that intentionally misplace harmful content away from the known high-risk coordinates.
core_contribution: The paper proposes SpatialJB, a jailbreak attack that reorganizes harmful text into 2D spatial layouts (six templates: Acrostic, Telestich, Center-Embedded, Staircase/diagonal, Corner Composition, and Multilingual Randomization) rather than a purely linear token sequence. The claimed insight is that Transformer self-attention operates on a serialized 1D token order, so spatially-adjacent (visually coherent) tokens that are sequentially distant lose attention coherence, which the authors formalize with two theorems (Theorem 1: self-attention has no direct access to visual neighbors outside serialized order; Theorem 2: expected attention weight decays exponentially with serialized token distance, E[A_ij] proportional to e^(-alpha|i-j|)). Because the text still reads coherently to a human, the harmful content survives while output guardrails (which rely on token/embedding-level semantic analysis) fail to flag it. The paper also proposes a baseline defense, SpatialD, which extracts tokens from fixed high-risk spatial positions (line starts/ends, corners, diagonal) and re-runs them through Llama Guard for a secondary safety check.
threat_model: Attacker interacts with a target LLM (and, in the guardrail experiments, a downstream output moderation layer) purely through prompting/API access -- no model weights, gradients, or fine-tuning access required. The attacker's goal is to elicit harmful content (hate speech / repeat or summarization of harmful text) that a human reader can still recognize as harmful, while the automated post-hoc output guardrail fails to flag it. Attack surface is the interaction between (a) the target LLM's willingness to reproduce/summarize spatially-reformatted harmful content and (b) a separate guardrail classifier (Llama Guard 4, OpenAI Moderation API, or Perspective API) applied to the model's output.
limitations: The paper's own theoretical claims (Theorems 1 and 2) are stated with proof sketches but are not empirically validated with controlled attention-weight measurements beyond a single illustrative pair of similarity-matrix figures (0.9419 linear vs. 0.5846 spatial local similarity) -- no systematic ablation isolates the theorem's predicted exponential-decay relationship from other confounds (e.g., prompt length, few-shot exemplar effects). ASR is judged by an unspecified success discriminator function J(x,r) whose reliability/human-agreement is not reported. The evaluated 'jailbreak' outputs are restricted to Repeat and Summary task framings, which is a narrower behavior space than open-ended harmful generation. The defense (SpatialD) explicitly relies on knowing the finite set of fixed template geometries used by the attack, so its generalization to novel/adaptive spatial layouts or to attacks that deliberately avoid the known high-risk coordinates is untested and flagged by the authors themselves as a weakness. Author-provided code repository is an anonymized link (not yet verifiable as a persistent public artifact). The paper is a preprint/anonymized submission (ACM template with placeholder venue/DOI, arXiv:2601.09321, cs.CR, dated Jan 2026) and has not undergone formal peer review at the time of this writing.

datasets_used: Dynamically Generated Hate Dataset (40,463 expert-annotated samples)
JailBench (used only for the SpatialD defense evaluation in Appendix B)
quantitative_results: SpatialJB Repeat-task ASR without guardrail: up to 100% on DeepSeek-V3/Llama4/Grok4, 91-97% on GPT-4, 89.7-94.3% on Gemini 2.5-Pro, 62.7-79.7% on Claude (Table 1)
Baseline jailbreaks (Zulu, Base64, Ubbi Dubbi) ASR: 4.7%-15.6% across all models/guardrails (Tables 1-2)
SpatialJB Repeat-task ASR under Llama Guard 4 for Claude (weakest target): 57-69.3% depending on template; under Perspective API: 61-77.7%
SpatialJB Summary-task ASR generally 15-40 percentage points lower than Repeat-task (e.g., Claude under Llama Guard 4: 42.8-52% Summary vs 57-69.3% Repeat)
Ablation on Claude (Table 3): Format Control alone = 68% ASR; + Positive Guidance = 86.80%; + Think Guidance = 76.20%; all three combined = 97.60%
Local semantic similarity (embedding-space): linear/1D layout = 0.9419, spatial/2D layout = 0.5846 (Figure 4)
SpatialD defense: Defense Success Rate averaged across 5 LLMs = 90.5% (Acrostic template), 87% (Telestich), 80.5% (Center), 81% (Corner), 77% (Staircase) (Table 4)
